Adına "Türkiye Yüzyılı" dedikleri masal için de
aynı durum geçerlidir ama "masal" lafı da bir latiflik yaratmasın. Anlattıkları masallar Türk milletinin er ya da geç kâbusuna dönüşmüştür. Hepimiz bu masal kahramanlarının kimler olduklarını biliyoruz. "Türkiye Yüzyılı" dedikleri şey, işte bu rakamların, bütçelerin yol açtığı karanlık tablodur; özünde ne Türkiye vardır ne de 21'inci yüzyıl. Müflis tüccarın iş kurma vaadi gibidir bu iktidarın hâli; içinde ne cumhuriyetin asarı ne de Türk milletinin hürriyeti ve istiklali vardır. (İYİ Parti sıralarından alkışlar)
Evet, bugün, bütün veriler bizi karamsar olmaya itmektedir ama sönmeyen bir ateş gibi, umut, bu milletin yüreğinde yeşermeye devam ediyor. Haklı ve güçlü gerekçelerimiz vardır. Aşılmaz denileni aşmışlığımız, başarılamaz denileni başarmışlığımız vardır. Bu topraklarda bu büyük milletin defalarca yazdığı büyük destanlar vardır. Elimizde Mustafa Kemal Atatürk'ün emaneti cumhuriyetimiz vardır. (İYİ Parti sıralarından alkışlar) Yeter ki Mustafa Kemal'in dediği gibi, tarih yazanlar tarihi yapanlara sadık kalsınlar; Türk milletini yönetenler yalnızca Türk milletine sadık kalsınlar. İşte, o zaman, değişmeyen hakikatimiz, cumhuriyetimiz ve istiklalimiz yeni yüzyılda kaldığı yerden insanlığı yeniden şaşırtacak bir mahiyette yolunu şan ve şerefle çizecektir.
Sayın milletvekilleri,
bilindiği gibi, Suriye'de olağanüstü gelişmeler yaşanmaktadır. Altmış bir yıllık zalim Esad rejimi düşmüş, Halep başta olmak üzere Suriyeli sığınmacıların yoğun olarak geldiği vilayetler rejim kuvvetlerinden arındırılmıştır. Suriyeli sığınmacıların Türkiye'de bulunmasına sebep olan şartlar fiilen ortadan kalkmış ve artık Suriyeli sığınmacıların Türkiye'deki varlık sebebi sona ermiştir. Türk milletinin talebi, hiç vakit kaybetmeden tüm sığınmacıların vatanlarına geri dönmesidir fakat akademik verilere ve tarihî vakalara göre uzun yıllar sonra gönüllü geri dönüş ihtimali son derece düşüktür. Dolayısıyla, İYİ Parti olarak bizim önerimiz, 2025 yılının ilk altı aylık döneminde gönüllü geri dönüşlerin teşvik edilmesi, 1 Temmuz 2025 tarihi itibarıyla Geçici Koruma Yönetmeliği'nin 11'inci maddesinin Hükûmete verdiği yetkiyle Suriyeli sığınmacılara sağlanan geçici koruma statüsünün iptal edilmesidir. (İYİ Parti sıralarından alkışlar) Sığınmacılara hiçbir şart altında vatandaşlık verilemeyeceği deklare edilmeli, Suriyelilere dağıtılan 238 bin vatandaşlık derhâl iptal edilmelidir. Suriyeli sığınmacılara tanınmış tüm ayrıcalıklar ortadan kaldırılmalıdır. Avrupa Birliğiyle para karşılığında yapılmış geri kabul anlaşması derhâl ama derhâl iptal edilmelidir. (İYİ Parti sıralarından alkışlar) Geçici koruma statüsünün iptal edilmesini müteakip kaçak duruma düşmüş tüm sığınmacıların ülkenin artık güvenli hâle gelmiş bölgelerine geri gönderilmesi esastır. Bu süreçte geri dönüşün sorunsuz, tam ve kâmil şekilde
icra edilmesi için Göç İdaresinin kurumsal kapasitesini artıracak bir eylem planı da ortaya konulmalıdır. Bu eylem planı bizde var. Eğer lütfeder, muhalefetle de görüşmeyi kabul ederseniz, Türkiye'nin temel meseleleri üzerinde çözüm önerilerini de paylaşmaya hazır olduğumuzu bilmenizi isterim. Her ne şart altında olursa olsun tüm Suriyeli sığınmacılar vatanlarına geri dönecek, PKK'nın Suriye'de devletleşme süreci bertaraf edilecek ve sınırlarımızın teröristan olması engellenecektir. (İYİ Parti sıralarından alkışlar)
Emperyalist güçlerin stratejik planları ve operasyonları, Orta Doğu'da yaşanan jeopolitik gelişmeler ve iktidarın öngörüden yoksun politikaları sonucunda Türkiye Cumhuriyeti devleti 1.289 kilometrelik güney sınır hattında önemli milî güvenlik riskleriyle karşı karşıya bırakılmıştır. 1991 Körfez Savaşı, Irak'ın kuzeyinde oluşan güç boşluğunu tetiklemiş ve merkezî otorite eksikliğinden kaynaklanan belirsizlik ve kaos, Türkiye'nin sınır ötesinde yeni bir tehdit oluşumuna zemin hazırlayarak bölgede terör ve tedhiş faaliyetlerinin artmasına sebep olmuştur. İşte tam da o dönemde Birleşmiş Milletler Güvenlik Konseyinin 5 Nisan 1991'de 36'ncı paraleli kapsayan 688 numaralı -güvenli bölge- Kararı, bu bölgedeki güç boşluğunu daha da derinleştirerek PKK'nın Irak'ın kuzeyindeki varlığını tahkim etmesine sebep olmuştur. Evvela zemin hazırlanmış, sonra emperyalizm eliyle 2003 yılında gerçekleştirilen
Irak işgali sonrasında bölgede 4 parçalı terör devletinin ilk adımı atılmıştır. Bu işgalin sonucunda 4 parçalı kürdistan projesinin Irak nezdinde 2 farklı varyantını oluşturan Irak Bölgesel Kürt Yönetimi ve PKK terör örgütünün bölgedeki gücü bilinçli olarak tesis edilmiştir. Emperyalistler, kukla terör devleti projesini tatbik edebilmek için, 2003'te Amerika Birleşik Devletleri işgalinden sonra hazırlanan Irak Anayasası'nda "2 toplumlu" yapı diyerek Irak'ı parçaladılar. Daha sonra, 25 Eylül 2017'de Irak'ın kuzeyinde yapılan sözde bağımsızlık referandumuyla bölgede ABD güdümünde 4 parçalı terör devleti kurma projesinin provasını yaptılar.
Değerli milletvekilleri, 1979 yılında, Amerika Birleşik Devletleri'nin Sovyetler Birliği'ne karşı Afganistan'ta desteklediği, Pakistan istihbaratı ve CIA desteğiyle medreselerde yetiştirdiği El Kaide, geçmişten bugüne kadar Batı'nın hedeflerini gerçekleştirebilmesi için bir araç olarak kullanılmıştır. Sadece Afganistan'ta değil, Irak'ta da El Kaide'nin kurulması 2003 yılında Amerika'nın Irak'ı işgalinden sonra gerçekleşecektir. 2004 yılında Irak'ın El Kaidesi kurulmuş ve bu "Irak El Kaidesi" denilen yapı Suriye savaşının başlamasıyla beraber 2012 yılında "El Nusra" adı altında Suriye'ye sızmıştır. İşte, bugün Türkiye Cumhuriyeti devletinin de terör örgütü olarak tanıdığı HTŞ aslında o El Nusra'dır
dolayısıyla da El Kaide'dir.
PKK-YPG elebaşı Mazlum Kobani'nin "Bizim HTŞ'yle ilişkilerimiz var ve biz HTŞ'yle hiçbir zaman savaşmadık." ifadeleri, PYD eş başkanı Salih Müslim'in "HTŞ'yle diyaloğa hazırız." ifadeleri bu 2 yapının eş güdümlü olarak hareket ettiğinin bilinen bir kanıtıdır. Geldiğimiz noktada, tıpkı dün Irak'ta olduğu gibi, bugün Suriye'de ülkenin birliği ve bütünlüğü parçalanmış ve Suriye'nin yüzde 40'ı PKK terör örgütünün kontrolüne geçmiştir.
Büyük Orta Doğu Projesi kapsamında Irak ve Suriye'nin parçalanması ve bu bölgelerde PKK'ya bağlı otonom bölgeler oluşturulması Türkiye Cumhuriyeti devleti için bir beka sorunudur. Mart 2011'de Suriye savaşının başlamasıyla beraber, merkezî otorite boşluğundan yararlanan PKK, Suriye'nin kuzeyindeki bölgeleri işgal etmeye başlamıştır. PKK, işgal ettiği bölgelerde stratejik göç mühendisliği yaparak Suriyeli Arapları ve Türkmenleri Türkiye'ye sürmüş, bir PKK devletinin nüfus altyapısını oluşturmaya çalışmıştır. Emperyalizm eliyle Suriye'nin neredeyse yarısını işgal etmiş olan PKK'nın batıda Akdeniz, doğuda Irak-Sincar bağlantısını kesecek bir müdahalede bulunmak zaruridir. Münbiç'ten başlayarak güvenlik sahasının Tabka, Rakka ve Haseke bölgesini kesecek şekilde genişletilmesi, Ayn el Arap'ın teröristlerden arındırılarak PKK devleti projesinin akamete uğratılması sağlanmalıdır.
Türkiye, Suriye'deki
savaştan en çok zarar gören ülkedir, diler ve umarım ki Suriye'deki barıştan da zarar görmez.
Ayrıca, bölgedeki Türkmen varlığı mutlaka korunmalıdır. Türkmenlerin yeni Suriye'nin kurucu unsuru olmaları ve anayasal bir statüye kavuşturulmaları tartışılmaz bir gerçekliktir. (İYİ Parti sıralarından alkışlar)
Türkiye'yi tarihî sorumluluklar beklemektedir. Türkiye Cumhuriyeti büyük bir devletin, Türk de büyük bir milletin adıdır. (İYİ Parti sıralarından alkışlar) İçinde yaşadığımız şu günlerde her zamankinden daha fazla birlik ve beraberliğe ihtiyacımız var. Yapılan hatalardan ders çıkarılmalı, emperyalist tuzaklara karşı da uyanık bulunmalıyız. Kimsenin bir karış toprağında gözümüz yoktur. İhtiyatlı ama aktif politikalarla millî güvenliğimizi korumak, vatan bütünlüğümüzü muhafaza etmek, hürriyet ve istiklalimizi garantiye almak mecburiyetindeyiz; bunun için verilmesi gereken her türlü doğru mücadeleyi vermeye kararlıyız, devletimizin ve milletimizin yanında olacağımızı da tüm dünyaya ilan etmekteyiz. (İYİ Parti sıralarından alkışlar)
Saygıdeğer milletvekilleri, aziz milletim; yeniden bütçeye dönelim. Bilinsin ki bu bütçe inşallah sarayın son bütçesidir, bir sonrakini yine inşallah millî Meclisimiz kendisi yapacaktır. Toplumun hiçbir kesiminin derdine çare olmayan bu zulüm bütçesine İYİ Parti olarak "ret" oyu kullanacağız. (İYİ Parti sıralarından alkışlar)
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
DURSUN MÜSAVAT DERVİŞOĞLU (Devamla) - Lütfeder misiniz Sayın Başkanım?
BAŞKAN - Bir dakika veriyorum.
Buyurun lütfen.
DURSUN MÜSAVAT DERVİŞOĞLU (Devamla) - Umutsuzluğa hiç gerek yok; Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisi gün gelecek, Türk milletinin kendisine verdiği yetkiyle dayatılan bu Düyun-ı Umumiye defterini geçmişte olduğu gibi fırlatıp atacaktır; Türk milletinin geleceğini yine bu büyük milletin azim ve kararı kurtaracaktır. (İYİ Parti sıralarından alkışlar)
Her şeye rağmen 2025 bütçesinin hayırlara vesile olmasını temenni ediyor, Komisyondaki görüşmelere katkı sağlayan tüm milletvekillerine, uzman kadrolara, grup çalışanlarımıza, bürokratlara, Meclis emekçilerine ve süreci takip ederek kamuoyuyla buluşturan basın mensuplarına şükranlarımı sunuyorum.
Sabrınız ve dikkatiniz için hepinize teşekkür ediyor, yüce Meclisi saygılarımla selamlıyorum. (İYİ Parti, CHP ve Saadet Partisi sıralarından alkışlar)
records.englishTranslation:
ON BEHALF OF GOOD PARTY GROUP DURSUN MUSAVAT DERVİŞOĞLU (Izmir) - Mr. President, esteemed Chairman of our political parties, esteemed MPs who strive to do justice to the greatness of our Assembly; On this occasion, I respectfully salute every individual of the great Turkish nation, the source of time, effort and sweat.
At the beginning of my words, I would like to express my condolences to the 6 soldiers who lost their lives in the helicopter crash in Isparta, and to their grieving families and our beloved nation.
Today, I am before you to explain my party's views on the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal and the 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal.
Those who are assumed to be present in this General Assembly hall are those named as the owners of sovereignty written on the wall behind us, that is, the nation itself. We are the ones responsible for asking what is spent and where in the budget. However, more than 90 percent of the central government budget is used directly by the executive body, that is, the Government. We know that there is only one person who gives instructions on how, where, to whom and for what purpose it will be spent.
The sole and real responsible for all expenditures and actions is not in the chair he should be sitting in today, as he has been for years. Unfortunately, he is far from respecting even the execution of the budget authority, which is the only and most important authority left in the hands of the Grand National Assembly of Turkey, whose many powers have been pruned, just as they have been far from the Turkish nation in spirit, heart and mind for a long time.
Mr. Yılmaz, please excuse me sir, we know you as a good bureaucrat, but this is the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye, that is, the national Assembly. Regardless of the temporary and artificial conditions of the current regime, the addressee of the National Assembly is directly the head of the executive, not his deputy.
Dear MPs, my dear nation; The budget is not a matter of today; It is a description of yesterday, today's situation, and a vision of what the future will be. Despite a government that only focuses on today, we are in a period where we must also focus on the future because the time we live in is very prone to deception and deception; Their interlocutors here know this well. The fact that we have been governed by a government that has substituted the margin of error and hitting the target interchangeably for twenty-two years, making these two concepts a transition between items,
Unfortunately, it makes the current century of risk much more threatening for Türkiye. That's why we are all obliged to make predictions more than just wishes. We have to find a common language of unity as a nation by distinguishing ideas from dreams. Time is working against us, and we cannot tolerate being deceived and deceived any longer. Türkiye can never keep up with the speed of time that flies by with this government that beats water in a mortar. While we were talking about the storm clouds that began to condense over the world and Türkiye a few years ago, today we are witnessing the breaking of that storm.
Humanity is tested in every field with a three-dimensional chess game; Undoubtedly, the usual balance of power also changes in this process.

The mode of production, financial and commercial relations, distribution and distribution forces every country to transform under the pressure of social and technological changes. As we leave the first quarter of the 21st century behind, we have moved from the internet era to the age of artificial intelligence. While the natural environment we live in is deteriorating, probably irreversibly, humanity stands on the verge of transferring or even transferring individual and cumulative consciousness to an artificial consciousness or artificial intelligence that is free from these environmental conditions.
Those who govern the Turkish state
It is questionable to what extent Türkiye can accurately read the changing balance of power in terms of its common interests, because most of our traditional red lines have been invalidated. If you remember, in the 90s, Türkiye struggled to protect its borders and integrity during the globalization period, in which the world allegedly became borderless, and it succeeded. Now, in the last twenty years, when borders have again become a matter of life and death for states and societies, Turkey has been deliberately led to the brink of cliffs by a government that has taken as its guide the most harmful aspects of a globalizationist view that was shelved even by its pioneers. In a period when sovereignty is once again concentrated in the nation-state, we have been exposed to risks that we have not encountered in a hundred years, as our nation-state sovereignty is constantly eroded by the current government. In the post-pandemic period, when the public has regained a say in production and distribution relations, we are left defenseless with an understanding that the most basic public services such as education and health are commercialized without rules. Considering that the legal order has moved away from even ensuring and maintaining the security of life and property, we are faced with an anarchic process of commodification rather than commercialization. in society
The factors that cause and nourish the general moral collapse should be sought here.
See, the competition between China and the United States continues neither only at sea, as in the 19th century, nor only in the field of intercontinental nuclear missiles, as in the 20th century. Therefore, we need to think carefully about what distinguishes yesterday's Cuba from today's Taiwan. In order to avoid being Turkey in the Cuban crisis that occurred in 1962 in a world on the verge of a future Taiwan crisis, our current position needs to be analyzed, classified and described very well, because yesterday's cold war is today's hot and cold war; In fact, what is happening is related to both local and global production and consumption chains; it is total, eclectic and unpredictable; It is as difficult to predict its ending as it is to mark its beginning. The effects of this war directly affect supply chains, trade and prices, and therefore daily life, within split seconds. Türkiye, on the other hand, thinks that it will be saved from these effects and reactions by the unfounded propaganda activities of a Directorate of Communications established with the totalitarian logic of the 20th century, and they want to be condemned to live in a dream world.

The huge problems we face are addressed by the Directorate of Communications.
With its ability to create and manage perception, it has turned into a frenzy of deception and deception, realities and dreams have become mixed together, but I say this from here: Enough is enough, the Turkish nation will no longer be deceived and deceived! (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
Dear MPs, my dear nation; One of the most crucial issues of today is the climate crisis. If we underestimate the consequences of climate change and prefer saving today to saving our future generations, we will have to face not only rising sea levels and drying dams, but also the largest migration waves in human history. In short, we will be overwhelmed by a migration crisis that is much bigger than the one we are experiencing today, because we have to decide now what to do tomorrow when large masses of people escaping from lands scorched by the climate crisis, waterless and barren lands, and stifling heat hit the migration paths, and our doors, borders and walls that we cannot close today will be tested tomorrow with a much larger and uncontrolled influx of those escaping from the climate disaster. While all sane governments in the world focus on reducing climate risks, accelerating green transformation, popularizing clean technologies, and increasing social resilience, we still prioritize narrow political interests, not the majority of the governed, but the ruling minority.
We see a budget that prioritizes comfort.
While the economy of the future is shaped around artificial intelligence, renewable energy, virtual currencies and high-tech products, we still see a government that calculates to whom and under what conditions the land rent will be distributed. While today's most powerful countries are looking for ways to protect their fertile lands and strengthen their agricultural production, we are still giving citizenship in exchange for land; We are still governed by an indifference that pours concrete into our plains and valleys and does not even care how resistant the concrete they pour is to earthquakes. I would like to ask: While even oil producing countries, from large to small, from Russia to Qatar, are steering their economies towards green transformation; While we, as Türkiye, have not yet been able to overcome the problems of our industrialists and the shortcomings of industrialization, how prepared are we for the green transformation that is at our doorstep? I wonder how much we can use the strategic value of the trade and energy routes and oil and gas lines passing through our lands? To what extent do we ensure the security, stability and sustainability of our energy resources, including nuclear energy, which we are too late for and which we want to boast about somehow finally owning?
Yes,
These are actually budget-related issues because they are all directly related to the unconditional sovereignty of the Turkish nation, but a power vacuum in which the method of handling all these issues is associated with the security of a person and his immediate environment, and the mentality gap created by that gap, leaves all these and hundreds of similar vital questions unanswered.
Dear MPs, my dear nation; Our republic, which has added one year to its 100th year, is losing "one more" of itself every day, with its institutions and individuals.

Türkiye's economic and social indicators; Especially when you look closely at the bazaar, market, school and hospital, the picture becomes even darker. This darkness is a reflection of the mentality that has forced our children and employees to wake up in pitch darkness instead of a bright morning when the sun rises for years. In the twenty-two years that have passed, the Public Procurement Law has turned into a sieve like our borders, and while the development plans and programs prepared by those who exempted themselves from the principles of the Public Financial Management and Control Law remained on the shelves, their own development plans were implemented. The commitments they made ten years ago; None of the targets of 2 trillion dollars national income, 25 thousand dollars per capita income, 500 billion dollars exports, 5 percent unemployment and single-digit inflation were achieved. Citizen tax burden
While being crushed under the pressure, all public resources were mobilized for the partisan institution. "Accountability" and "fiscal transparency" have become concepts that remain only on paper, the Court of Accounts has been rendered dysfunctional, the Parliament's budget right has been constantly usurped, and even the decisions of the Constitutional Court have become unrecognizable. Clearly, degradation and decay are not just matters of today; This process has accelerated in an unprecedented way with the presidential system of government.
Türkiye's economy has been dragged into disaster under the leadership of a person who claims to be an economist. Minister Mehmet Şimşek, who was appointed to the post after the 2023 elections, has actually confessed this fact, which we have expressed many times on this platform, one by one. He has personally confirmed firsthand that the policies followed are irrational, that our net reserves have gone negative, that the budget deficit will reach 10 percent of the national income if no measures are taken, and many other mistakes.
I'm asking: Exactly twelve years ago, you set 37 targets for the 100th anniversary of the Republic of Turkey, I congratulate you, you could not achieve 34 of them (!) (Applause from the ranks of the İYİ Party) Inflation would fall to single digits, 64.8 percent, which was the highest inflation rate of your period of power.
You caught it in 2023. The share of high technology products in exports would be 20 percent; While the share of high technology in exports of manufacturing industry products was 3.1 percent in 2011, you could not even increase it to 4 percent. "The unemployment rate will drop to 5 percent." You said, you finished 2023 with 9.4 percent, it looks like you will finish this year around 9 percent. "Unregistered employment will decrease to 15 percent." You said, it was 26 percent. "500 billion dollars of exports." You said, you're only halfway through. "Per capita income is 25 thousand dollars." You said, but you could only see $13,243. "The population below the poverty line will decrease." You said, while there were 21 million 730 thousand people below the average income in 2011, you added 3 million more in 2023.
"Women's labor force participation rate will reach 38 percent." You said, but you have been able to increase it to 35 percent or you are about to increase it. I am saying these, please forgive me, Mr. Vice President, I do not want to force it, but while saying all these, I also remember this: "Mothers should not focus on any other career other than their motherhood career.

" so
I would like to take this opportunity to remember a former Minister of Health who made a statement. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
Moreover, when we look at the targets you presented for 2028 in the Twelfth Development Plan, we see that you have admitted with your own hands that what you promised for 2023 cannot be realized even in 2028. "It will be 2 trillion dollars in 2023." The latest plan for the gross domestic product you mentioned is targeted at 1.59 trillion dollars in 2028; per capita income is $17,554, unemployment rate is 7.5 percent; exports are determined as 375 billion dollars; The share of high technology products in exports will be 5.5 percent. These will be nothing more than a set of empty dreams, which are embarrassing compared to the 2023 targets, but which are unlikely to come true as long as this understanding of politics continues.
Before I forget, besides these promises, you also had another promise; a hard landing on the moon this year was among your promises. Yes, your landing is quite harsh, but the place you land on is not the moon; You are falling from power, you don't realize it. (Applause from the ranks of İYİ Party and Felicity Party)
Dear MPs, we are together today to discuss the 2025 budget, but this budget before us is no different from an accounting book imposed without allowing it to be discussed. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches) This is me
This is my 7th budget in the Parliament after becoming a parliamentarian. Thousands of documents come from the palace as per the system, discussions are held in the Commission for days, and then thousands of pages of minutes are kept. As far as I know, his working hours this term have exceeded two hundred and fifty-five hours.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - Two hundred and thirty-five hours.
DURSUN MÜSAVAT DERVİŞOĞLU (Continue) - But in all fairness, let's succeed in changing at least one number or one letter of this budget in the Commission for seven years! Otherwise, the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye is treated as a group of people working for free and wasting time.
When viewed today, the budget is no different from other budgets imposed and this budget, like the ones before it, is a picture of waste and disorder. However, accounting cannot hide bankruptcy. Bankruptcy is related to the situation in which Türkiye has been reduced. The Turkish citizen fell from the 1st league, which should have been in the world league, to the 3rd league. While a handful of minorities live like oil sheikhs, the remaining 80 million suffer from lawlessness, injustice and inequality.
I know you don't care much about these numbers, but let's look at the table: While we were ranked 59th in the Rule of Law Index in 2014, we will be ranked 117th in 2023. In the Global Peace Index
While we were ranked 128th in 2014, we will be ranked 139th in 2024. While we were ranked 53rd in 2013 in the Corruption Perception Index, we will be ranked 115th in 2023.
I am happy to welcome your smile, Mr. Group President.
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Sivas) - I will give information.
DURSUN MÜSAVAT DERVİŞOĞLU (Continuing) - Of course you can.

While it was ranked 69th in the United Nations World Happiness Report in 2017, it rose to 98th in 2022, from 66th in the Wellbeing Index in 2011 to 95th in 2023, from 83rd in the Human Freedom Index in 2010 to 128th in 2023, and from 60th in the Economic Freedom Index in 2011. While we ranked 138th in 2022 and 102nd in the Press Freedom Index in 2008, we have fallen to 158th place in 2024. I know you don't take these numbers seriously, I guess you must have an answer to them as well.
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Sivas) - Yes, sir.
BÜLENT KAYA (Istanbul) - The work of foreign powers (!)
DURSUN MUSAVAT DERVİŞOĞLU (Continuing) - But the situation is so dire that they said to the camel, "Why is your neck crooked?" and he said, "What's the truth?" he said.
ABDULLAH GÜLER (Sivas) - I hope you will not be embarrassed.
DURSUN MÜSAVAT DERVİŞOĞLU (Continuing) - I want to tell you what is right and where you will correct it. (Applause from the ranks of İYİ Party and Felicity Party)
May God help us all, this country belongs to all of us. We are in the same boat but in different seas, it seems. (Applause from the ranks of İYİ Party and Felicity Party)
While these data were in evidence, Mr. Şimşek traveled around the world looking for investors with a program he called "medium-term program". Looking at these rankings, it has always been a matter of curiosity for me as to which sane investor and which clean money will come to our country. Of course, the expected happened and Türkiye was faced with the worst of money and investment. The resources of our country, which had been exploited for years by the palace and its cronies, would be given away to some foreign investors. You always say "We are attracting foreign investors." You say, that's what I'm talking about; When confidence in the economy disappeared and there was no predictability, we could not even keep the investments of our own citizens in the country, let alone attract foreign direct investment.
If you want, should I compare it with the past, should we go back to the period we lived in with the black plow, should we talk about those periods when there was no Justice and Development Party, that is, when the world was a cloud of gas and dust? In the period from the last quarter of 2001 to the last quarter of 2002, the ratio of net foreign direct capital investments to national income was 0.61 percent; Today, when we look at the data of the last four quarters, this rate has decreased to 0.45, and if we exclude real estate sales, this rate drops to 0.22 percent. What does this mean? A company that goes no further than real estate
It means vision. This government, which enriched its own palace minority with cheap loans by reducing interest rates as of September 2021, would this time increase interest rates and make the "carry traders", which is the polite word for usurers, happy. Of course, an issue related to the happiness of farmers whose crops are not profitable, retirees trying to make ends meet, tens of millions of workers forced to earn minimum wage, or students going to school hungry could not be an issue for Mr. Şimşek and his program.
Dear MPs, the fundamental transformation that the world is going through is not only based on balance of power, geopolitical moves, energy agreements and commercial rivalries.

At the same time, economic models are changing on a global scale, the acceleration of technology and the forced course imposed by the climate crisis bring with it new rules. However, our country is not ready for this great transformation, and it is transferring the dilemmas from the 20th century, deepening them, to the middle of the 21st century. The wrong policies that the government has been pursuing for years, and the management approach consisting of sad strategies that are certainly not deep, have turned Türkiye into a runner trying to catch up with the global trend but falling down every time, instead of putting it into a rapid adaptation process. Today, Türkiye has neither strengthened its industry nor modernized its agriculture.
It appears to be a country that has neither effectively adapted to the digital age nor been able to renew its social contract. We continue to be engulfed in deep crises caused by sad strategies ranging from law to social justice, from education to the immigration problem. You may say: "In order to get out of this situation, we need reform, a new social consensus, a new constitution that will provide an umbrella for this, and a long-term vision." However, the only reality we encounter under the name of "reform" in twenty-two years of power is the legal acrobatics and maneuvers carried out so that Mr. Erdoğan can run for the Presidency again. Where is the reform? Where is the constitutional transformation? Where is a political, economic and social reconciliation text suitable for the conditions of the 21st century? Unfortunately there isn't. We are faced with an understanding that uses the Constitution as a toy to prepare a special ground for itself and cannot make this country a roof suitable for everyone.
Leaving aside the fact that you continue the national defense moves that started after the Cyprus Peace Operation, is there any story to be proud of in return for all this loss? The government, which has been talking about the goal of contemporary civilization for decades, dragged the country, let alone contemporary civilization, to a worse point than when it was taken over, when faced with the cold face of real data.
if you want
Let's go back to old Türkiye again, that is, the era of the black plow, that is, the era of the black tire.
In November 2002, when the AK PARTY came to power, according to TÜİK data, consumer inflation was 31.8 percent, that is, this inflation rate had actually disabled the then government in the elections and brought it below the threshold. At the same time, while TURKSTAT determined this rate as 31.8 percent - between the figures of TURKSTAT and the inflation determined by other inflation platforms, when the scissors were not open - that is, according to ITO, this rate was 31.3 percent. The economic program already implemented at that time was steadily reducing inflation. Regardless of who came to power, inflation was already expected to fall below 10 percent by all economists.
Well, what about today? According to TUIK's updated figures, annual inflation is 47.1 percent; According to ITO, it is 58 percent. Prices are 4.39 according to TUIK data in the last three years; According to ITO data, it increased by 5.65 times. This simple indicator alone shows that you have managed to make Türkiye worse than when your government took over the country.

At the end of twenty-two years, the point we have reached in terms of inflation is obvious: Citizens are suffering from inflation that has eroded their purchasing power and evaporated their income.
You left us alone with a monster and brought the inflation monster back to life; You have created an economy of immorality.
Dear Members of Parliament, what will you say about foreign direct investments, which is described with great excitement and whose advertisements are in full force in the newspapers? There may also be those who say. Do you call the coming of Chinese automotive companies to Türkiye, which will not produce parts in Türkiye, provide employment, but only for their own benefit and take a share of the market, a foreign investment?
The government has an unreasonable approach to capital and human movements. Chinese automotive companies are coming to Türkiye, and unfortunately Turkish textile and clothing companies are going to Egypt. Qualified Turkish human resources to the USA and Europe, unqualified Syrian and Afghan fugitives to Türkiye. These would be the only things that would happen if this country were governed under a mandate and protectorate regime.
TurkStat considers our citizens who graduated in the 2008-2017 period and stayed abroad for at least three years between 2018-2022 and definitely in 2023, as having migrated abroad as of 2023. Have you looked at the rates of your higher education graduates in the 2008-2017 period who met this criterion? 18 percent of genetics graduates;
9.1 percent of electronics engineering graduates; 8.4 of computer engineering graduates; 7.8 of software engineering graduates; 7.6 percent of economics graduates. I think we all know that these people are the best in their fields and that almost all of them are young people who graduated from universities with the highest grade point averages. As you said to our doctors before, "Let them go if they go." will you say? Let them go and will you carry out Türkiye's green transformation, shoulder its digital revolution and establish a high-tech industrial infrastructure with the Syrian geneticists, Afghan engineers and Somali software developers you have poured into our borders? When the most qualified people leave and are replaced by those who are even below the global average, we are drifting towards below average level in the country. When the most qualified economics graduates migrate abroad, managing the economy is left to relatives, Mr. Nebati and Kavcıoğlu, that is, people like you. Dear MPs, the qualified brain drain caused by the AKP's mandate regime is the reason, and the situation of our country, which has been dragged below par in every field, is actually the result. (Applause from the ranks of the İYİ Party and the Saadet Party) Well, in this budget, in the previous budgets, we addressed the human resources problem.
Are there any signs of intent to resolve? I looked but couldn't find it, it doesn't exist. Why are young people leaving? Because you do not recognize the right to life for anyone other than yourself, you either label anyone who makes a noise as a "terrorist" or throw them in prison as a "criminal". When freedom of expression is trampled on, young people cannot find space for themselves.
While we ranked 115th in the World Bank's Voice and Accountability Index in 2002, we fell to 150th place in 2023. At this point, we are compared to the rates in Mali and Nicaragua, that is, our competitors in this process are Mali and Nicaragua.

There are military coups in Mali and the oppressive practices of the Ortega regime in Nicaragua. So, what's in Türkiye? In Türkiye, there is a twenty-two-year-old Justice and Development Party government and palace sultanate, a state administration that has no sense or brain left, and of course, that is why there is a brain drain. What else? There is injustice in every field, there is social injustice, there is unemployment, there is despair. In an environment where our broadly defined unemployment rate has increased to 25.6 percent, we have people who cannot find a job, have lost hope, and are not even looking for full-time jobs. I say these with a heavy heart. Look, I say this with all my sincerity that I am not expressing all this just to criticize. But Türkiye needs to be aware of these facts and make this country
That's why I'm talking about the fact that the rulers need to learn from these and put themselves in order. (Applause from the ranks of the İYİ Party and the Felicity Party) This rate increased by 9.3 points compared to the previous freak system in 2018. For those who find work, the country is a minimum wage hell, and with it a tax hell.
Minimum wage, as its name suggests, is a base wage that should provide a minimum standard of living. In our country, at least 42 percent of our employees earn minimum wage, and 51 percent earn a salary of 10 percent more or less than the minimum wage. The minimum wage, which is the starting level for employment and is actually an exception, has become a general practice in Türkiye. So no matter how you look at it, you cannot offer at least half of our people enough wages to live the decent life they deserve. While this minimum wage is being discussed, it is tragicomic that the Central Bank managers defend an increase rate compatible with the 2025 inflation targets and encourage the business world to demand low rates. We have seen which target you have achieved, and now you will adapt the minimum wage to that target.
We are discussing the minimum wage, but those in power do not discuss the minimum wage, they determine it. I would like to draw your attention here: The name of the commission is "Minimum Wage Determination Commission." but in reality
Dried cakes, orange juices, mixed nuts and accompanied by "the commission for approving the figures of the palace" is the name of this Commission. (Applause from the ranks of the İYİ Party) The place to really determine the minimum wage is the streets. Oh, brother, don't you ever meet ordinary people, for God's sake? The citizen has no teeth in his mouth, no teeth left in his mouth, no teeth left to chew a bite, no shoulders left to wear a cardigan; You are still waiting for citizens to understand you. In other words, while you are eating buffalo yoghurt, Medina dates, chestnut honey and going to bed, you are taking the citizens' savings as taxes and expecting them to save. (Applause from the ranks of the İYİ Party) Türkiye has been imprisoned in the middle income trap for years, and the middle class has been chained to the minimum wage; You, on the other hand, do not and cannot even save money on corruption.
Well, in this budget, budgets and budget items, can we see traces of moves that will keep our youth and qualified human resources in the country, and measures that will finance digital and green transformation processes? Unfortunately no. While 9.4 per ten thousand of the budget was allocated to the youth program in the first ten months of 2024, this rate drops to 7.8 per ten thousand in 2025.

So, you think that our young people are satisfied and that we do not even need to invest in them.
The same goes for employment programs. Employment program share of 3.4 percent in 2024
It drops to 2.2 percent in 2025. While the signals of economic cooling, company bankruptcies, concordats, closed businesses, and spreading unemployment are evident, you are reducing the share of these programs. What does this mean? Despite the changing global competitive conditions, you cannot even make the necessary public investment to transform and qualify our workforce. You don't care about anyone's preparation for changing world conditions because dealing with these issues before the election approaches is actually not in your interest.
Did you say fight against poverty? In 2024, 2.58 percent of total program expenditures were going to fight poverty. This year, this rate increased to 2.83 percent, signaling some progress, but in 2025, you reduce this rate again to 2.51 percent. I ask: Why? Then it is predicted to increase again in 2026 and 2027. Why should it decrease in 2025 and increase in 2026? I wonder about this. Of course, when you look at it from here, you stand out as a government that encourages and reinforces poverty, not one that fights corruption and poverty. Because you are a government that adjusts the poverty problem according to the election calendar. Your head is spinning with the pleasure of managing despair, poverty and addiction. That's why
You can index people's pain and despair to election history and survey data. Today, 1 in 3 children lives in severe financial poverty, 7 million children grow up in poor households, and 4 out of 10 children in the 0-14 age group are poor. Our children experience developmental disorders due to lack of nutrition, and most of these children live in the Southeastern Anatolia Region, where the population growth is highest. However, when you say "Southeast", you only think of the politics of terrorism, feeding ethnic politics, but not feeding poor and hungry children. While the children in that region are struggling for work, education, health and welfare, you are seeking political gain. Unfortunately, you cannot see that these regions are condemned to poverty and that the future of the country depends on investing in these children. In the 2025 budget, I could not see any steps, strategic planning or reforms to improve human resources on this issue. If what you call "normalization" is really going to happen, it will only be possible by solving these problems. If there is a problem that needs to be solved, this is it; if there is someone who needs to be helped, it is these children; if there is a situation that needs to be brought back to normal, it is this situation of shame.
My dear nation, valuable members of parliament; We are against this budget because this budget
It is a budget of oppression that does not meet any needs for the Turkish nation, on the contrary, it does not contain the right things for the Turkish nation, and has emerged contrary to the expectations of the Turkish nation. (Applause from the ranks of the İYİ Party) This budget is the ostentatious budget of the people sitting in the offices of the state; This budget is a budget that is far from the founding principles of the Republic of Türkiye and does not deal with constitutional obligations; It is a budget far from justice, transparency and equity.

While the government constantly talks about savings measures, luxury official vehicles and wasteful projects continue, citizens' access to basic services is restricted. The 2025 budget is a budget that reinforces waste rather than savings; We know that there are much more than the items we see. This budget is the budget of those who seek prestige in vanity projects, not in social projects or the welfare of individuals.
The tax system is unfair and oppressive. A total revenue of 12.8 trillion lira is predicted for the 2025 budget; Approximately 11.2 trillion liras of this will be provided from tax revenues, but 66 percent of these tax revenues consist of indirect taxes, that is, the citizen pays the price of this unfair system every time he shops, every time he buys fuel, every time he puts a bite of bread on the table. So, what is the share of direct taxes, that is, income and corporate taxes? Only 30 percent. This rate
It is the opposite in developed countries. As always, the taxes that should be collected from the rich fall on the poor in this country. You cannot solve this country's economic problems without reforming the tax system; You cannot change anything without reducing indirect taxes or increasing the share of direct taxes. "We will reduce tax exemptions." you said; There are also tax exemptions for delivery to the address in the savings package, but there is no savings. There is no tax on the rich, those who make excessive profits, and those who survive with cheap loans. We are hoping for this structural reform, but it is not in this budget either.
I call out from here: This injustice is not sustainable. Injustice is everywhere. It is especially among the duties of the two most basic and vital Ministries; in national education and health. Equality and accessibility of education determines the future of a country, but the share allocated to education in the 2025 budget is 1.45 trillion lira; This corresponds to approximately 10 percent of the total central government budget. Isn't it beautiful? In fact, it has increased, but Dear Government officials, this budget has increased, but there is no soap and no cleaning staff in schools. Inequality of opportunity in education is getting deeper day by day. Public schools struggle with lack of resources, and the financing of education falls on parents and families.
continues. The national quality and constitutional nature of national education is virtually being destroyed. The quality of the Republic that produces a President from a peasant is being destroyed because of this system. Health, on the other hand, is now in the lap of mafiaization rather than commercialization. The budget allocated to health expenditures is equally deceiving as to education. Huge costs have been imposed on the nation with city hospital projects. The healthcare sector has been commoditized. Health has become a trade in one's body and life rather than a human right or a citizen's right. This is not privatization and marketization. This is a direct threat to life, and it is building a system that is hostile to both doctors and patients. "We built a school." You said, there is no education in it; "We built the palace of justice." You said, there is no justice in it; "We built a hospital." You said, it's not about health, it's about profiteering gangs and profiteers who don't even hesitate to kill newborn babies. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches) "We built a bridge." You said, you took it from the one whose money was not worth it.

In our opinion, the 2025 budget will not be able to satisfy this ravenous profit monster. Türkiye's food inflation is among the highest in the world, but the budget allocated to agricultural support is far from supporting production. While our farmers struggle with high costs, production is declining due to import-based policies.
The 2025 budget again lacks a vision that will nurture rural development and agriculture. The government does not even consider fulfilling its constitutional duty. Dear members of the government, this country cannot stand up without supporting its own producers; We have to increase production and reduce foreign dependency in agriculture. This is a security issue and, in fact, a future issue for all of us. As you know, the 2024 budget was made with the same mentality, and the 2025 budget is unfortunately a continuation of the same mentality and the same fiction.
The 2025 budget is a budget of oppression. In this tyrannical budget, there is an allowance for tenderers, interest, institutions that are made losses, and those who make money from money, but there is no allowance for retirees. The 2025 oppression budget is again a budget that takes from the poor and gives to the rich, a budget that focuses on inputs, not results. "We allocated this much to education and this much to health." they say, "What result did you achieve?" When we ask, we cannot get an answer.
There is unemployment in the 2025 cruelty budget, or if there is anyone who says there is no unemployment, come here and say it; There is despair in this budget. The 2025 cruelty budget includes exploiting the feelings of the nation and currying favor with global monopolists. There is no room for structural reforms in the 2025 oppression budget, there is no additional tax on the rich and supporters in the 2025 oppression budget. There are no farmers, no students in the 2025 cruelty budget. There is no savings in the 2025 persecution budget. Now, will we call this budget the "welfare budget"?
This budget is, of course, a budget of cruelty and will go down in history as such. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
I am complaining about you to the nation. You have squandered this nation's rights and laws; You have condemned this great nation to poverty; You took the farmer away from his field, the livestock farmer from his pasture, and the peasant from his home; You caused the tradesmen to come to the point of closing their shutters with foreclosures and enforcements; You destroyed the hopes of young people, darkened their future, and condemned them to wait in visa queues; You will both go and give account of your actions. This nation will not forgive you its rights, I hope God Almighty will forgive you. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
Dear MPs, as we move into the 20th century.
CEMAL ENGİNYURT (Istanbul) - In the account section.
DURSUN MÜSAVAT DERVİŞOĞLU (Continuing) - Even your words from there are enough to confuse me.
CEMAL ENGİNYURT (Istanbul) - Let it be in between, President, you continue to hold him accountable.
DURSUN MÜSAVAT DERVİŞOĞLU (Continuing) - Thank you.
Dear MPs, my dear nation; We lost a lot of time and were too late in transitioning to the 20th century. As we enter the second quarter of the 21st century, history repeats itself. The fact that the government renders meaningless and invalid everything that it expresses and uses as material for its propaganda, such as local and national, democracy, Constitution, and all our sacred things that I cannot even begin to repeat here, are twenty-two years of constant diseases, a disease that turns into character.

For the tale they call "Türkiye Century"
The same situation applies, but the word "fairy tale" should not create any pleasantries. The fairy tales they told turned into the nightmare of the Turkish nation sooner or later. We all know who these fairy tale heroes are. What they call the "Türkiye Century" is the dark picture caused by these figures and budgets; In essence, there is neither Türkiye nor the 21st century. The state of this government is like a bankrupt merchant's promise to start a business; It contains neither the principles of the republic nor the freedom and independence of the Turkish nation. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
Yes, today, all the data push us to be pessimistic, but like an unquenchable fire, hope continues to grow in the hearts of this nation. We have just and strong reasons. We have overcome what was said to be insurmountable, and we have achieved what was said to be unachievable. There are great epics written many times by this great nation in these lands. We have our republic, entrusted to us by Mustafa Kemal Atatürk. (Applause from the ranks of the İYİ Party) As long as, as Mustafa Kemal said, those who write history remain loyal to those who make history; Those who govern the Turkish nation should remain loyal only to the Turkish nation. Then, our unchanging truth, our republic and our independence will chart its path with glory and honor in the new century, in a way that will surprise humanity once again.
Dear MPs,
As it is known, extraordinary developments are taking place in Syria. The sixty-one-year cruel Assad regime has fallen, and the provinces where Syrian refugees come heavily, especially Aleppo, have been cleared of regime forces. The conditions that caused Syrian refugees to be in Türkiye have practically disappeared and the reason for the existence of Syrian refugees in Türkiye has now ended. The Turkish nation's demand is for all refugees to return to their homeland without delay, but according to academic data and historical cases, the possibility of voluntary return after many years is extremely low. Therefore, as İYİ Party, our recommendation is to encourage voluntary returns in the first six months of 2025, and to cancel the temporary protection status provided to Syrian refugees as of July 1, 2025, with the authority given to the Government by Article 11 of the Temporary Protection Regulation. (Applause from the ranks of the İYİ Party) It should be declared that citizenship cannot be granted to refugees under any circumstances, and the 238 thousand citizenships distributed to Syrians should be canceled immediately. All privileges granted to Syrian refugees should be eliminated. The readmission agreement made with the European Union in exchange for money should be canceled immediately. (Applause from the ranks of the İYİ Party) It is essential that all refugees who have become illegal following the cancellation of temporary protection status be sent back to the regions of the country that have now become safe. In this process, the return will be smooth, complete and perfect.
An action plan that will increase the institutional capacity of the Immigration Administration should also be put forward for its implementation. We have this action plan. If you would kindly agree to meet with the opposition, I would like you to know that we are ready to share solution proposals on Türkiye's fundamental issues.

Under any circumstances, all Syrian refugees will return to their homeland, the PKK's nationalization process in Syria will be eliminated and our borders will be prevented from being invaded by terrorists. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
As a result of the strategic plans and operations of the imperialist powers, the geopolitical developments in the Middle East and the government's short-sighted policies, the Republic of Turkey has been left facing significant national security risks along the 1,289-kilometer southern border line. The 1991 Gulf War triggered the power vacuum in the north of Iraq, and the uncertainty and chaos resulting from the lack of central authority paved the way for the emergence of a new threat beyond Türkiye's border, causing an increase in terrorism and terror activities in the region. At that very time, the United Nations Security Council Resolution No. 688 - safe zone - covering the 36th parallel on April 5, 1991, further deepened the power gap in this region and caused the PKK to consolidate its presence in the north of Iraq. First, the groundwork was prepared, then the actions taken by imperialism in 2003
After the invasion of Iraq, the first step of a four-part terrorist state was taken in the region. As a result of this occupation, the power of the Iraqi Regional Kurdish Administration and the PKK terrorist organization, which created 2 different variants of the 4-part Kurdistan project in Iraq, was consciously established in the region. In order to implement the puppet terrorist state project, the imperialists dismembered Iraq by calling it a "bi-communal" structure in the Iraqi Constitution, which was prepared after the United States invasion in 2003. Later, with the so-called independence referendum held in the north of Iraq on September 25, 2017, they rehearsed the project of establishing a four-part terrorist state under the control of the USA in the region.
Dear MPs, Al-Qaeda, which was supported by the United States in Afghanistan against the Soviet Union in 1979 and trained in madrasahs with the support of Pakistani intelligence and the CIA, has been used as a tool for the West to achieve its goals from past to present. The establishment of Al Qaeda not only in Afghanistan but also in Iraq would occur after the American invasion of Iraq in 2003. Al Qaeda in Iraq was established in 2004, and this structure called "Al Qaeda in Iraq" infiltrated Syria under the name "Al Nusra" in 2012, with the beginning of the Syrian war. Well, HTS, which is recognized as a terrorist organization by the Republic of Türkiye today, is actually Al Nusra.
hence Al Qaeda.
PKK-YPG leader Mazlum Kobani said, "We have relations with HTS and we have never fought with HTS." The statements of PYD co-chair Salih Müslim, "We are ready for dialogue with HTS." These statements are known evidence that these two structures act in coordination. At this point, just like in Iraq yesterday, the unity and integrity of the country has been torn apart in Syria today, and 40 percent of Syria has come under the control of the PKK terrorist organization.
The disintegration of Iraq and Syria within the scope of the Greater Middle East Project and the establishment of autonomous regions affiliated with the PKK in these regions is a survival problem for the state of the Republic of Türkiye.

With the start of the Syrian war in March 2011, the PKK, taking advantage of the gap in central authority, began to occupy the regions in northern Syria. By carrying out strategic migration engineering in the regions it occupied, the PKK deported Syrian Arabs and Turkmens to Türkiye and tried to create the population infrastructure of a PKK state. It is necessary to make an intervention that will cut off the PKK, which has occupied almost half of Syria with the hands of imperialism, the Mediterranean connection in the west and the Iraq-Sinjar connection in the east. Starting from Manbij, the security area should be expanded to include Tabqa, Raqqa and Hasakah region, Ayn al Arab should be cleared of terrorists and the PKK state project should be foiled.
Türkiye in Syria
It is the country that suffered the most from the war, and I hope that it will not be harmed by the peace in Syria.
Additionally, the Turkmen presence in the region must be protected. It is an indisputable reality that Turkmens are the founding elements of the new Syria and have been given a constitutional status. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
Historical responsibilities await Türkiye. The Republic of Türkiye is the name of a great state, and Turk is the name of a great nation. (Applause from the ranks of İYİ Party) In these days we live in, we need unity and solidarity more than ever. We must learn from the mistakes made and be alert against imperialist traps. We have no eyes on an inch of anyone's land. We are obliged to protect our national security, preserve the integrity of our homeland, and guarantee our freedom and independence with cautious but active policies; We are determined to give every right struggle necessary for this, and we declare to the whole world that we will stand by our state and nation. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
Dear MPs, my dear nation; Let's get back to the budget. Let it be known that this budget is the last budget of the palace, hopefully the next one will be made by our National Assembly itself. As İYİ Party, we will vote "rejection" to this cruel budget, which does not solve the problems of any segment of society. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
DURSUN MÜSAVAT DERVİŞOĞLU (Continuing) - Could you please, Mr. President?
CHAIRMAN - I'll give you a minute.
Here you go please.
DURSUN MÜSAVAT DERVİŞOĞLU (Continuing) - There is no need to despair; The day will come when the Turkish Grand National Assembly will throw away this Public Debt Registry, which was imposed with the authority given to it by the Turkish nation, as it did in the past; The future of the Turkish nation will be saved by the determination and decision of this great nation. (Applause from the İYİ Party benches)
Despite everything, I hope that the 2025 budget will be beneficial, and I would like to express my gratitude to all MPs, expert staff, group employees, bureaucrats, Parliament workers and members of the press who followed the process and brought it to the public.
I thank all of you for your patience and attention, and I respectfully greet the Supreme Assembly. (Applause from the ranks of İYİ Party, CHP and Saadet Party)

records.originalTurkish:
İYİ PARTİ GRUBU ADINA DURSUN MÜSAVAT DERVİŞOĞLU (İzmir) - Sayın Başkan, siyasi partilerimizin Saygıdeğer Genel Başkanları, kıymetli milletvekilleri, ekranları başında bizleri izleyen sevgili vatandaşlarım; 2026 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Bütçe Kanunu Teklifi ve 2024 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Kesin Hesap Kanunu Teklifi'yle ilgili olarak İYİ Partinin görüş ve düşüncelerini açıklamak üzere
söz almış bulunuyorum. Hepinizi en samimi duygularımla selamlıyorum.
Sözlerimin başında, sabah saatlerinde gerçekleşen bir saldırı sonucu şehit olan Özel Harekât Polisi kardeşimiz Emre Albayrak'a ve tüm şehitlerimize Cenab-ı Allah'tan rahmet diliyorum.
Görüşülen bütçe aynı zamanda bir yılın siyasi muhasebesidir. Bu sebeple, konuşmama dünyamızı, bölgemizi ve ülkemizi etkileyen fay hatlarını ele alarak başlamak istiyorum. Bugünün dünyasında dramatik değişimlere tanık oluyoruz. Soğuk savaş sonrasında hâkim olan geçici iyimserlik geçmişte kalmıştır. Küreselleşme kavramı ise etkileri ve sonuçlarıyla sorgulanmaktadır. Evrensel norm olarak kabul görmüş birçok ilke de itibarsızlaşmıştır. Uluslararası hukuk kolayca göz ardı edilmektedir. Küresel ticaret koruma duvarlarıyla sınırlandırılmaktadır. Böyle bir atmosferde doğal olarak güvenlik kaygıları yükselmektedir. Çatışma alanlarının en uzağında olduğu var sayılan devletler bile askerî harcamalarını artırmaktadır. Diplomatik kural ve teamüller yok sayılmaktadır. Böylece, yaşanan tüm gerilimler kolayca tırmanmakta, sıcak çatışmaları önlemek ve sınırlamak daha da zorlaşmaktadır. Ancak tüm bu tabloyu küreselleşmenin sona ermesi diye okumak doğru olmayacaktır. İkili bir düzenden hatta bir ikilemden bahsedilmesi gerekmektedir çünkü tüm bunlar olurken küresel ticaret büyümekte, enerji, teknoloji ve madencilik başta
olmak üzere yeni iş birliği ağları örülmektedir. Öte yandan, küreselleşmenin olumsuz etkileri de sürmektedir. Terör, kitlesel göç, dijital manipülasyon ile çevre ve iklim sorunları tüm coğrafyaları etkilemektedir. Açıktır ki tehditler daha asimetrik hâle gelmiştir ve ülkeler kendilerini bu tehditlere karşı korumak için çareyi yeniden ulus devlette aramaktadırlar. Ancak neoliberal tahribat neticesinde geri dönen, geriye dönülen çoğunlukla baskı ve zor aygıtlarıdır. Bu çelişkili ve çetrefilli dönemde dış politikanın iç politikaya tahvil edildiğine tanıklık etmekteyiz. Dış politikada emperyalizmin ve irredantizmin, iç politikada ise despotizmin ve otokrasinin yeni biçimlerini görmekteyiz. Amerika Birleşik Devletleri'nden Çin'e, Rusya'dan Türkiye'ye, İtalya'dan Macaristan'a kadar farklı düzeylerde benzer durumlar söz konusudur. Kısaca, güvenlik riskleri güvenlik ihtiyaçlarını beslerken aşırı güvenlik arzı da demokratik kurumları ve süreçleri daha da sakatlamaktadır. Bu kapsamda, dünyanın yeniden tarihsel bir kısır döngüye kapılma ihtimali artmaktadır. Zira güçlü, istikrarlı ve rasyonel yönetim talebi arttıkça bu hükûmetlerin ellerindeki gücü hoyratça kullanma ihtimalleri de artıyor; belirsiz, tekinsiz ve kaygılarla yüklü toplumlar manipülasyona çok daha açık hâle geliyorlar. Güvenlik gerekçelerinin
arkasına sığınılarak denge ve denetim mekanizmaları bertaraf ediliyor. Dolayısıyla böyle zorlu zamanlar ülkeler için bir yol ayrımı niteliği taşır. Reel politik anlayışın yeniden yükseldiği bu dünyada duygusallığa, ideolojik takıntılara, hayalperestliğe, kimlikçiliğe, hamasete, romantik heveslere, değerli yalnızlıklara, derinlikli fantezilere yer yoktur. İktidardan beklenen, fayda-maliyet analizini doğru yapmasıdır. Muhalefete ve bir bütün olarak millet aklı ve iradesine düşen ise iktidarın şahsi ve siyasi hesaplarının karar alma süreçlerini belirlemesine izin vermemektir. Bu açıdan, Türkiye'nin asıl imtihanı budur. Şimdiye kadar, üzülerek söylüyorum ki Türkiye'yi yöneten iktidar uluslararası sistemdeki dönüşümleri ve güvenlik ihtiyaçlarını defalarca istismar etmiştir. Konuşmamın sınırları bakımından tek bir örnekle yetineceğim. Düşen Rus uçağının üstüne kutusunu bile açamadığımız S-400 savunma sistemini satın alarak parasını ödediğimiz F-35 projesinden dışlanmamız bunların başında gelmektedir. O günlerde âdeta tek koruyucumuzun S-400 sistemi olduğu söyleniyor, yapılacak teknoloji transferleri anlatılıyordu. Neticede, 8 Aralık 2025 tarihi itibarıyla elimizde ne kullanabildiğimiz gelişkin bir hava savunma sistemi ne de aldığımız ya da ürettiğimiz bir savaş uçağı yoktur. Üstelik, F-35 projesinden çıkmamızın asgari maliyeti
15 milyar dolardır. Bunları niye hatırlattım? Kaynak arıyorsunuz ya, işte size buhar olmuş bir kaynak örneği.
Devam edeyim. Şimdi, İsrail tehdidinden bahsediyorsunuz. Bakıyoruz, İran'ı yüzlerce kilometre öteden F-35'lerle vuruyor, Ege ve Akdeniz'de Yunanistan'ın şeytani emellerine işaret ediyorsunuz. O da F-35'lerine güveniyor. Peki, siz ne yapıyorsunuz? Amerika Birleşik Devletleri'ne "Bari F-16 ver." diyorsunuz, bunun için de sayısız kapitülasyonların yanında 30 milyar dolarlık yolcu uçağı almayı taahhüt ediyorsunuz. Medyanızda İngiltere'yi "her taşın altındaki şeytan" diye taşlatıyor, sonra da onların başarısı oldukça şüpheli olan savaş uçaklarını almak için 11 milyar dolarlık anlaşma yaparak misliyle para veriyorsunuz.
Soruyorum: Tüm bunlar ulusun güvenliğini mi yoksa iktidarın güvenliğini mi sağlamak içindir? Sırf buraya kadar vergi mükelleflerinin sırtına yüklenmiş ve buhar olan 50 milyar dolardan bahsediyorum. Eğer başka türlüsünü yapabilseydiniz bugün bu bütçe görüşmesinde "geliştiriliyor" diye hepimizin övündüğü yerli silah sistemlerimizin çoktan Silahlı Kuvvetlerimize yapılan teslimatları konuşulacak, dost ve kardeş ülkelere ihracatının getirilerinden bahsetmiş olacaktık. Bugünse bize kalan, boşa harcanan paralar ve geri gelmeyecek tam on yıllık zaman.
Ortaya çıkan risk ve kırılganlığı hiçbir
iletişim çalışması telafi edemez. Yarın, Allah korusun, büyük bir çatışma çıkarsa "Kusura bakmayın, 2030'a kadar savaşamayacağız." mı diyeceksiniz, mola mı isteyeceksiniz? Türkiye, bekası üzerine zar atılacak bir memleket değildir. Bu vatanı uzak akrabanızdan miras kalan tarla olarak görmekten artık vazgeçmenizi istiyoruz.
Güvenlik riskinin böylesine yüksek olduğu bir dünyada, Türkiye'de Hükûmetin böyle irrasyonel adımlar atma, böyle fahiş hatalar yapma lüksü asla yoktur. Milletin karşısına çıkıp oy isterken kendilerini ulusal güvenliğin ve bekanın teminatı olarak sunanların onu böyle hoyratça harcamasının mantıklı bir açıklaması da yoktur.
Bugün, vatandaş olarak hepimize ait ortak servet olan beytülmalı konuşmak için toplanmış bulunuyoruz. Yüzyıllardır olan ve olması gereken şey, parayı harcayan kim ise paranın asıl sahibine hesap vermesidir; Parlamentonun varlık gerekçesi de budur. Gelin görün ki harcamanın sorumlusu olan Sayın Cumhurbaşkanı, bütçeyi sunmak üzere, milletin seçilmiş vekillerine kendi tayin ettiği yardımcısını yollayarak her zamanki gibi bugün de Meclisi teşrif buyurmamışlardır. Orta Doğu'nun meşruiyet pazarından takviye aramak yerine devlet ve millet düsturuna saygı duyarsanız başka yerlerde meşruiyet pazarlıkları yapmak zorunda kalmazsınız. Tekerleme yaptığınız ama
anlamını unuttuğunuz yerlilik böyle bir şeydir, millîlik ise milletin Meclisinden başlar. O sebeple bugün burada iktidarın programdan çok propaganda olarak gördüğü ekonominin sadece rakamlarını değil, her gün yüzümüze tokat gibi çarpan hakikatlerini ele almak üzere konuşacağım ve konuşmamı da mümkün olduğu kadar bütçenin sınırlarında kalarak gerçekleştirmeye çalışacağım.
İktidar kürsülerde sürekli bekadan, güvenlikten ve büyüyen Türkiye'den bahsediyor; doğrudur, ilgili rakamlardan da bu büyüme fazlasıyla görülüyor. Ancak büyüyenin yurdum insanının rızkı ve umudu olmadığı kesindir. Ballandıra ballandıra anlatılan aslında toplasanız ancak bin odayı dolduracak kadar kişinin servetidir. O bin hanenin servetinin kaynağı ise 26 milyon hanenin yani 85 milyon vatandaşın hakkı ve emeğidir. Bu gasp rejimini de Yeni Türkiye ambalajıyla sunuyorsunuz. Kurduğunuz bu rejimin adı "yeni" değil "eşitsiz Türkiye"dir; eşitsizliğin bedelini ödeyen ise tükenen, tüketilen Türkiye'dir. Bu sebeple bütçenizin ismi de bize göre "tükeniş bütçesi"dir.
Alt alta sıralanan bol sıfırlı rakamların, rengârenk tabloların hiçbiri çeyrek asırlık yetersizliğinizi, sekiz yıldır derinleştirerek yönettiğiniz sürekli krizi gizleyemiyor. Çünkü hakikat, korkuyla ve baskıyla susturulmak istenen Türkiye'nin sessiz ve öfke dolu çığlıklarındadır.
Siz artık o çığlıkları duyamayacak hâldesiniz. Hakikat, siftahsız esnafın, güvencesiz çalışanın, istikrarsız piyasanın hakikatidir. Hakikat, maalesef ve maatteessüf sahipsiz Türkiye'dir.
Bütçe teklifinde Hükûmet 19 trilyon liralık devasa bir harcama yetkisi istiyor. Geçtiğimiz sekiz yılda olduğu gibi, serveti harcayan ile serveti harcanan arasında hiçbir ilişki bulunmuyor. 13,8 trilyonluk vergi gelirinin kaynağı vatandaş, bütçenin en büyük kara deliği ise 2,7 trilyon liralık faiz gideridir. Faiz giderinin bütçeye oranı on yılda neredeyse 2'ye katlanmıştır. Yani daha en başından 2026 bütçesinin yedide 1'i yatırım ve üretim yerine faiz lobilerine aktarılacaktır. Netice ise yüksek bütçe açığı, yüksek maliyetli borçlanma, yüksek enflasyon ve alım gücünün çöküşüdür.
Emekliye gelince "Kaynak yok.", öğretmene gelince "bütçe yükü", asgari ücretliye gelince "Enflasyon artar." denmektedir; sıra sarayın harcamalarına gelince tasarruf söz konusu olmadığı gibi kaynak sorunu da yaşanmamaktadır. Önümüze gelen bu tablo bir bütçe cetveli değil topuzu kaçmış bir vicdan kantarıdır.
Bir de nereye gittiği belli olmayan örtülü ödenek meselesi hortlatılmıştır. 2026 yılında bu ödenekten harcanacak paralarla kaç yangın
uçağı alınabilir, kaç depremzedeye ev yapılabilir, kaç çocuğa öğle yemeği verilebilirdi? İktidar, buna da "devlet sırrı" adını takmıştır. Cumhurbaşkanlığına ayrılan bütçe ve örtülü ödenek öngörülerini alt alta koyduğunuzda korkunç bir tablo ortaya çıkıyor: 2026'daki günlük harcama 60 milyon lira yani saatte 2,5 milyon, dakikada ise 41 bin liradır. Buna göre sarayın her dakikası için 2 asgari ücretli tam bir gün bir ay çalışmak durumundadır.
Bakanlık bütçelerine Tarım ve Orman Bakanlığından yani ekmeksiz Türkiye'den başlıyorum. Hükûmetin bu alanda görünen tek hedefi çiftçinin tasını tarağını toplayıp tarlasını terk etmesidir. Tarım Kanunu'nun 21'inci maddesinde "Tarımsal desteklemeler millî gelirin yüzde 1'inden az olamaz." ibaresi bulunmasına rağmen 2026 için öngörülen oran sadece binde 2'dir. Kanun "yüzde 1" diyor, ortada gıda krizi var, siz binde 2 veriyorsunuz. Sadece 2026 yılında çiftçinin cebinden gasbettiğiniz destek miktarı 604 milyar Türk lirasıdır. Esirgediğiniz destek miktarıyla yol açtığınız zararın farkında bile değilsiniz. Üretim çöktü, çiftçi borç batağında. Çiftçinin kredi borcu geçen yıla oranla yüzde 43 artarak 1 trilyon 130 milyar liraya ulaştı. Son 20 yılda çiftçinin geliri 1 lira, borcu ise 7 lira artmıştır.
Buğday ekim alanlarımızın yüzde 27'sini kaybettik.
Nüfusumuz 2000'den bugüne yüzde 26 artarken kişi başına düşen buğday üretimi yüzde 22 azalmıştır. Vatandaş et yiyemiyor, et; üretici maliyetler yüzünden anaç hayvanlarını kesime gönderiyor. Gıda enflasyonunda dünya şampiyonluğuna oynuyoruz. OECD ülkelerinin ortalaması yüzde 5 iken Türkiye'de yüzde 27. İnşaata gelirsek; her biriniz mücahitken, maşallah, müteahhit oldunuz. Ancak, çeyrek asırdır bir şantiye rejimiyle idare edilen, beton dökülmeyen, imara açılmayan yeri kalmayan Türkiye'de vatandaşlar başlarını sokacak yuva bulamıyorlar. İnsanımız evsizdir. 2002 yılında, hane halkının ev sahiplik oranı yüzde 73 iken, bugün yüzde 55'e düşmüştür. Elinizdeki rakamlar konut sayısının arttığını yazıyor ama kurduğunuz rant düzeni millete yuva kurmayı değil, emlak baronlarına stok sağlamayı hedefliyor. Konut, Türkiye'de zenginlerin hobisine dönüşmüştür. Ev sahibi olanlar evsizler değil 2'nci, 3'üncü, 5'inci evini alanlardır.
"Deprem kader, mülk Allah'ın." diyerek deprem evleri yapıyorsunuz, yaparken de bağdakini dağa kovuyorsunuz, gözü kara bir cüretle tapulara el koyuyorsunuz, "cumhuriyet tarihinin en büyük sosyal konut hamlesi" diye kürsülerden müjdeler veriyorsunuz. Rakamlar ise başka bir hikâyeyi anlatıyor, yarattığınız derin yoksulluğu ve çaresizliğe haykırıyor. Bu bir başarı tablosu değil bir iflas belgesidir, politikalarınızla yarattığınız evsiz Türkiye gerçeğinin ta kendisidir.

barınma hakkını şans oyunlarına çevirdiniz. 2022'de 8 milyon vatandaşımızın başvurduğu İlk Evim Projesi'nde de aynı şeyi yaptınız, siz "Arsalar hazır." dediniz, vatandaş başvurduğunda ise "Henüz tahsis edilmiş arsa yok." yanıtını aldı.
